mnemonics: FCDP - First Come, Different Processing (FIFO, Circular, Deque, Priority)
keyPoints: Circular Queue: Connects the front and rear to efficiently reuse space in a fixed-size buffer.
Double-Ended Queue (Deque): Allows insertion and deletion from both the front and rear.
Priority Queue: Processes elements based on priority rather than order of arrival.
commonMistakes: Assuming all queues are strictly FIFO (ignoring Priority and Deque variants).
Not utilizing the modulo operation for indices in a Circular Queue, leading to index overflow errors.
Implementing a Priority Queue as a simple linear list instead of a more efficient heap structure.
